title: Unlimited Token Approval to Untrusted Addresses
reason: For factory-created pools the numeraire and reserve addresses are identical, so the `safeApprove` branch is not taken; the remaining concern is a deployment-time trust assumption rather than an exploitable bug in the shipped factory path.

title: Owner Can Permanently Freeze Protocol Functions
reason: This is an explicit privileged control, not a coding flaw; freezing blocks swaps but does not by itself create unintended fund loss, and proportional withdrawal remains available unless emergency mode is separately enabled.

title: Whitelisted Depositors Can Withdraw More Than Deposited
reason: The code does not enable double-withdrawal; after transferring LP away, the recipient is the one that gets stuck by whitelist underflow, while the original depositor still cannot burn more LP than they hold.

title: Missing Parameter Validation Allows Dangerous Pool Configuration
reason: `setParams` already enforces bounds on alpha, beta, fee-at-halt, epsilon, and lambda; the residual risk is ordinary owner/governance authority, not missing validation.

title: Division by Zero When Pool Has Zero Total Supply
reason: With zero total supply there is no outstanding LP-backed claim to redeem; this is only a zero-amount edge-case revert, not a realistic permanent lockup scenario.

title: Flash Loan Fees Sent to Owner Instead of Protocol Treasury
reason: This is an economic/design choice about fee recipient, not an unintended exploit path that lets an unprivileged attacker steal or lock protocol funds.

title: Oracle Price Manipulation Through External Dependencies
reason: The report is generic and not supported by this codebase: `Storage.oracles` is declared but not wired into the pool pricing paths reviewed here.

title: Hardcoded Merkle Root Cannot Be Updated
reason: An immutable whitelist root is a design choice; by itself it does not create realistic theft, insolvency, or lockup beyond the intended whitelist policy.

title: Deadline Check Uses Incorrect Comparison Operator
reason: Using `<` instead of `<=` only affects transactions mined at the exact deadline timestamp and is a minor UX edge case, not a reportable security issue.

title: CurveFactory Allows Creating Duplicate Pools with Same Currency Pair
reason: The factory rejects duplicate ordered pairs and no deletion path is present; the claim about recreating deleted pools is unsupported by the code shown.
